query: Show me time conversion routines in SPICE
answer:
The SPICE Toolkit contains several routines for time conversion. They include `NSPCHT`, `FMTTIM`, `SCE2T`, `SCE2C`, and `SCT2E`.

- `NSPCHT`: Computes the width of a time format string, handling both SCLK and "normal" time formats [file:26-234]. It checks for the leapseconds kernel file and SCLK kernel file [file:81-90, 115-121].
- `FMTTIM`: Converts an epoch to a string, supporting SCLK formats and standard time formats like UTC, JED, ISO, and ISODOY [file:36-185]. It also checks for required kernel files [file:62-71, 93-99].
- `SCE2T`: Converts spacecraft clock time to ephemeris time (ET) [file:3-3
